codeReview.summary: The rerun fixes most of the prior review's concrete findings: the root OAuth/server regression suites are now tracked, the webhook missing-sheetId regression truly omits request sheetId, and the touched webhook tests use innocuous fake literals. However, the feature still does not satisfy its remaining scope requirement that the claimed OAuth/session and local service boundary hardening be landed in this feature's own commit set.

issues.description: The rerun adds regression coverage, but the feature still does not own the promised OAuth/session and local service boundary hardening. `git show --stat 43b8226` adds only the two new root tests plus webhook-test updates, while `git blame app.js:6211-6345` attributes the OAuth session boundary logic to earlier commits `5f12a17c`/`4e8a4849` and `git blame server/security-boundaries.mjs:1-60` attributes the local service origin/scrape-target hardening to `cf9aa04c`. Because this feature's commit set remains `ac86145` + `43b8226`, the explicit expected-behavior requirement that this feature land those hardenings in feature-owned commits is still unmet.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The integration-refactor worker skill still lacks the mission-approved dirty-baseline and temporary-port verification exceptions, so the latest successful worker had to report `followedProcedure: false` for behavior that mission guidance explicitly allows.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `integration-refactor-worker/SKILL.md` step 6 requires verification on the real local surface and does not mention temporary-port fallback, and the skill omits the dirty-baseline exception entirely. In the latest handoff (`42b5329f-a8b8-4207-9b05-8125a719e54e`), `skillFeedback.deviations` calls out using port 3947 because 3847 was occupied and leaving unrelated baseline artifacts untouched; both patterns are explicitly allowed by mission `AGENTS.md` and `.factory/library/user-testing.md`.

summary: Fail: the rerun fixes the prior test-tracking, placeholder-literal, and missing-sheetId coverage issues, but the feature still does not land the claimed OAuth/session and local service hardening in its own commit set, so the feature description remains only partially satisfied.
